scenario: Aurora Retailer attaches routing labels when creating order-process instances in Camunda 8.8 across 9000 orders per day. The platform team plans tag values like channel:web and region:emea, and needs to confirm the validation rules the orchestration cluster applies to tag values during process instance creation.
question: What are the documented constraints on process instance tags supplied at creation time in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: A maximum of 10 unique tags per instance, each 1 to 100 characters, starting with a letter and matching the case-sensitive pattern, with no edits allowed after creation
A maximum of 25 unique tags per instance, each 1 to 200 characters, starting with any alphanumeric character, with values editable through the Operate web UI after creation
An unlimited number of tags per instance, each 1 to 50 characters, starting with a digit or letter, with full wildcard search and edits allowed throughout the instance lifetime
A maximum of 5 unique tags per instance, each 1 to 64 characters, starting with a letter or digit, mutable through the REST API and shown by default in Operate and Tasklist

a.text: Correct. The documented limits are at most 10 unique tags per instance, length 1 to 100, starting with a letter and matching the case-sensitive pattern, and tags cannot be modified after creation.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented maximum is 10 tags of length 1 to 100, tags must start with a letter, and tags are not shown or editable in the Operate web UI.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented cap is 10 unique tags of 1 to 100 characters, tags must start with a letter, and partial or wildcard matching is not supported by search.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented limit is 10 unique tags of 1 to 100 characters, tags are immutable after creation, and they are not shown by default in Operate or Tasklist.

explanation: Process instance tags are immutable, 1 to 100 character strings that start with a letter and match a case-sensitive pattern, capped at 10 unique values per instance and validated at creation.
